items.name: Windows 11
items.description: Primary OS for daily work, .NET dev, and most everything else.

items.name: Fedora
items.description: Linux side — clean, current, and a great match for server-style work.

name: EDITOR & CODING
subtitle: Where most of the actual work happens.

items.name: Visual Studio Code
items.description: Primary editor for web, scripting, and quick edits across languages.

items.name: JetBrains Rider
items.description: Goes here for serious .NET work — refactors, debugging, profiling.

items.name: Claude Code
items.description: AI pair-programmer in the terminal. Used heavily for refactors and reviews.

items.name: Windows Terminal + PowerShell
items.description: Default shell. Tabs, profiles, and a sane font stack.

items.name: Git + GitHub CLI
items.description: Version control and PR workflows straight from the terminal.

name: LANGUAGES & RUNTIMES
subtitle: The platforms I build on day to day.

items.name: .NET / C#
items.description: Backend APIs, worker services, and SignalR realtime stacks.

items.name: Node.js
items.description: Tooling, scripts, and the Next.js side of every project.

items.name: Python
items.description: AI/RAG pipelines, FastAPI services, OpenCV, and data work.

name: FRAMEWORKS & LIBRARIES
subtitle: The toolkits I reach for first.

items.name: .NET
items.description: Primary backend platform — APIs, worker services, and realtime stacks.

items.name: FastAPI
items.description: Python's most ergonomic API framework — typed, async, fast.

name: DATA & INFRA
subtitle: Storage, caches, and the plumbing behind the apps.

items.name: PostgreSQL
items.description: Default relational store. Boring, predictable, powerful.

items.name: Redis
items.description: Caching, locks, signaling fanout, ephemeral state.

items.name: MongoDB
items.description: For document-shaped data and quick prototypes.

items.name: Docker
items.description: Consistent local environments and deploy artifacts.

items.name: Cloudflare
items.description: DNS, CDN, and edge bits that keep the site quick.

name: PRODUCTIVITY
subtitle: Notes, tasks, and keeping things from falling through the cracks.

items.name: Notion
items.description: Long-form notes, project docs, and personal wiki.

items.name: GitHub Projects
items.description: Lightweight issue and roadmap tracking next to the code.

items.name: Obsidian
items.description: Local-first markdown notebook for quick capture.

name: BROWSER & WEB
subtitle: Where the rest of work happens.

items.name: Brave
items.description: Primary browser. Fast, private, Chromium-compatible.

items.name: Chrome DevTools
items.description: Profiling, network inspection, and the occasional console hack.

name: AUDIO PRODUCTION
subtitle: What I use when making and mixing music.

items.name: FL Studio
items.description: Primary DAW for production, sequencing, and mixing.

items.name: iZotope
items.description: Goes here for mixing, mastering, and audio repair — Ozone, Neutron, RX.

name: HARDWARE
subtitle: The physical kit I sit in front of every day.

items.name: ThinkPad E14
items.description: Intel Core Ultra 5, 32 GB RAM. Daily driver for everything.

items.name: BenQ GW2790Q
items.description: 27-inch 2K (1440p) IPS display. Easy on the eyes for long sessions.

items.name: Keychron K3 Pro
items.description: Low-profile mechanical keyboard. Compact, quiet, and built for long typing sessions.

items.name: Razer DeathAdder V2 X HyperSpeed
items.description: Wireless gaming mouse. Comfortable shape, reliable for both work and play.

items.name: Beyerdynamic DT 990 Pro
items.description: Open-back studio headphones. Wide soundstage, comfortable for long listens.

items.name: Focusrite Scarlett Solo
items.description: USB audio interface. Clean preamp for monitoring and recording.
